lineage.decision: Conditional demand, common rows, global WAPE
lineage.reason: Every candidate is scored on the same purchasable product-days.
It beat the recursive route and avoids feedback compounding.

lineage.decision: Guarded log-residual around a 4:3:2:1 weekday anchor
lineage.reason: The anchor carries stable weekly level; the network learns departures.

lineage.decision: Three-seed PyTorch network with product and campaign embeddings
lineage.reason: This was the strongest defensible non-tree specification before Chronos-2 was introduced.

models.label: Best NN
models.blurb: The strongest specification developed in the original project: a direct seven-horizon PyTorch network that learns a guarded log-residual correction around a same-weekday seasonal anchor.

models.label: Chronos-2
models.blurb: Amazon's pretrained time-series foundation model, evaluated zero-shot on exactly the same rolling origins, products, target dates, scoring population and future-known retail information.

output_hashes.reason: Artifact hashes authenticate the retained files now but were not all committed immutably at model-run completion.

verification.reason: The run enabled --resume before checkpoint source/input/dependency/device identities and checkpoint hashes were bound into the immutable manifest. The local execution log reports zero reused folds and 19 trained folds, but that observation was not authenticated at run time.

source.reason: Publication-only hardening occurred after model inference. These file hashes bind the exact publication source without claiming that the later source produced the model forecasts.
